description:
You are acting as a build engineer who has been asked to capture a repeatable, 
   self-contained snapshot of the machine’s health so that the CI/CD pipeline 
   can archive it alongside a nightly build.  

   Your assignment consists of three sequential phases:

   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   1. Create the destination area
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   • Make sure the directory  
     /home/user/build_artifacts/diagnostics  
     exists (create all intermediate directories if they do not already exist).  

   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   2. Collect diagnostics and write them to a single log file
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   • Inside /home/user/build_artifacts/diagnostics create a plain-text file 
     named system_diagnostics.log.  
   • That file MUST follow *exactly* the skeletal format shown below.  
     - Every header line must appear exactly as written (case-sensitive, no 
       leading or trailing spaces).  
     - The blocks between START and END tags must NOT be empty.  
     - Lines may wrap naturally, but do not insert blank lines except where 
       explicitly shown in the template.  

     ==============================================================
     # Build Diagnostics Report
     
     ### DISK_USAGE_START
     (output of a single df or lsblk style command goes here)
     ### DISK_USAGE_END
     
     ### MEM_USAGE_START
     (output of a single free -m style command goes here)
     ### MEM_USAGE_END
     
     ### PROCESS_LIST_START
     (output of a single ps -eo pid,comm,pcpu,pmem --sort=-pcpu style command goes here)
     ### PROCESS_LIST_END
     
     ### OPEN_PORTS_START
     (output of one ss -tulnp OR netstat -tulnp call goes here)
     ### OPEN_PORTS_END
     
     # End of Report
     ==============================================================

     Notes on acceptable content:  
     • You may add as many data lines as the underlying command produces,  
       but you MAY NOT delete, rename or reorder the header/footer lines.  
     • Make sure each “START” header appears on its own line, immediately 
       followed by one or more lines of command output, then the matching 
       “END” header on its own line.  

   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   3. Package the artifacts
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   • Create a compressed tarball named  
     /home/user/build_artifacts/build_diagnostics.tar.gz  
     that contains *only* the diagnostics directory (i.e. the tarball must 
     unpack to build_artifacts/diagnostics/system_diagnostics.log).  
   • Leave the original diagnostics directory and its log file intact on disk.  

   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   Verification expectations (read carefully)
   --------------------------------------------------------------------
   1. The directory /home/user/build_artifacts/diagnostics must exist.  
   2. The file /home/user/build_artifacts/diagnostics/system_diagnostics.log  
      must exist and fulfill ALL of these conditions:  
      a. First line is exactly “# Build Diagnostics Report”.  
      b. Last line is exactly “# End of Report”.  
      c. The eight header/footer tags appear in this precise order:  
         1) ### DISK_USAGE_START  
         2) ### DISK_USAGE_END  
         3) ### MEM_USAGE_START  
         4) ### MEM_USAGE_END  
         5) ### PROCESS_LIST_START  
         6) ### PROCESS_LIST_END  
         7) ### OPEN_PORTS_START  
         8) ### OPEN_PORTS_END  
      d. Each START/END pair encloses at least one non-empty, non-whitespace 
         data line.  
   3. The tarball /home/user/build_artifacts/build_diagnostics.tar.gz must exist  
      and, when listed with tar -tzf, must contain exactly one file path:  
      build_artifacts/diagnostics/system_diagnostics.log  

   The automated grader will fail the task if **any** of these conditions are 
   not met, so pay close attention to the required file structure and header 
   alignment when generating your output.
truth:
Expected filesystem entities after successful completion:
   1. Directory: /home/user/build_artifacts/diagnostics  (mode 755 or more permissive)
   2. Log file:  /home/user/build_artifacts/diagnostics/system_diagnostics.log
      • Must contain 12 mandatory lines in fixed positions:  
        Line  1: # Build Diagnostics Report  
        Line  2: (blank)  
        Line  3: ### DISK_USAGE_START  
        Line  5: ### DISK_USAGE_END  
        Line  6: (blank)  
        Line  7: ### MEM_USAGE_START  
        Line  9: ### MEM_USAGE_END  
        Line 10: (blank)  
        Line 11: ### PROCESS_LIST_START  
        Line 13: ### PROCESS_LIST_END  
        Line 14: (blank)  
        Line 15: ### OPEN_PORTS_START  
        Line 17: ### OPEN_PORTS_END  
        Last line: # End of Report  
      • Lines 4, 8, 12, 16 must each contain at least one character other than 
        whitespace (the actual command output is flexible).  
   3. Tarball: /home/user/build_artifacts/build_diagnostics.tar.gz
      • tar -tzf /home/user/build_artifacts/build_diagnostics.tar.gz  
        must output exactly one path:  
        build_artifacts/diagnostics/system_diagnostics.log  

   The grader will check:  
   • Existence and readability of all entities.  
   • Exact matching of required header/footer lines (string equality).  
   • Non-emptiness of command output regions via regex “^\s*\S+” detection.  
   • Tarball content list equals 
     [ "build_artifacts/diagnostics/system_diagnostics.log" ] exactly.
